verbatim_text: We're going to insert the activation vector into the language model as a continuous token in the embedding layer. And then we're going to minimize this negative log probability. Basically, this is just the SFT objective. OK, so what are the results of this training? Does this result in better self models? And specifically, does this result in the two parts of the definition we saw earlier? Self-consistency and privileged access. So we measure self-consistency in several ways. The one we're going to just plot here is going to be whether the language model generated description matches the ground truth description from the interpretability tools. And we're going to compare language models before and after being trained to explain themselves across the three types of questions that we just saw. So here we see the results and we see indeed they can produce highly accurate self descriptions after being trained to explain themselves. OK, so yes, models can be trained to produce self-consistent explanations. Now, how about privileged access? So in order to measure this, we want to compare a model that's trained to explain itself against another model that's trained to explain the first model.
